expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: modern grid hyperlink-style coverage separates contextual-link styling from ordinary element rendering
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly addresses the separation: it states two categories—“Cells/objects with contextual links → must render with hyperlink styling and indicator icon” vs “Ordinary grid elements without contextual links → … render as normal (non-hyperlink) content,” and proposes separate Phase 4a subcategories/scenarios for each (“contextual-link styling” vs “ordinary (non-link) styling” plus “Mixed-content … differentiation”).

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: result.md frames the verdict as “phase_contract • advisory,” references “Alignment with Phase 4a contract (primary checkpoint),” and lists Phase 4a structure requirements (“subcategory → scenario → atomic action chain → observable verification leaves” and “forbids canonical top-layer categories”). It also includes a “Phase alignment check” noting the primary phase under test is phase4a and limits claims due to missing runtime artifacts.
